name: Amr ibn Khalid
title: The Loyal Martyr
shortDescription: A sincere Shia from Kufa who, under Ibn Ziyad's strict siege, escaped the city at night and joined the Imam in Karbala.
fullStory:
Amr ibn Khalid al-Saydawi was a sincere, brave, and well-known Shia in Kufa. He had a special devotion to the household of the Prophet (PBUH). When Muslim ibn Aqeel rose up in Kufa, Amr was one of his supporters, but after Muslim's martyrdom and Ibn Ziyad's strict control over Kufa, he went into hiding.

When the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) arrival in Karbala spread, Amr decided to reach the Imam at any cost. Along with his servant (Sa'd) and a few others including Majma' ibn Abdullah al-A'idhi, they escaped Kufa at night, evading Ibn Ziyad's patrols. Guided by Tirimmah ibn Uday, they navigated through off-road paths and reached Karbala.

Hurr ibn Yazid al-Riyahi, who was still in the Kufan army at that time, tried to stop them from joining the Imam, saying: 'These people have come from Kufa and must be arrested or returned'. But Imam Hussain (AS) firmly said: 'I will protect them just as I protect my own life; they are my companions'. With the Imam's resistance, Hurr gave up on arresting them.

On the day of Ashura, Amr ibn Khalid al-Saydawi, along with the friends who had come with him from Kufa, launched a collective attack on the enemy army. They were surrounded by the enemy, and their connection to the tents was cut off. Imam Hussain (AS) sent his brother Abbas (AS) to rescue them. Abbas broke the siege with Haidari bravery and saved them.

However, Amr and his companions, filled with the desire for martyrdom, upon seeing their rescuer (Abbas), charged back into the heart of the enemy army and fought until they all attained martyrdom in one place together. Their collective martyrdom was a tableau of friendship and brotherhood in the path of Wilayah.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عَمْرِو بْنِ خالِدٍ الصَّيْداوِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Amr ibn Khalid al-Saydawi.
